classification_rationale: This is the AFI documentation hub. README.md:6 states it is "the official documentation hub for every public facet of AFI: protocol specs, developer guides, governance blueprints, and the illustrative Signal-Lore series." AGENTS.md:13-16 explicitly forbids code ("What this repo is NOT for: Code implementation (use afi-core, afi-reactor, etc.)"). The tree contains only Markdown/JSON config (no .ts/.sol sources). Notably it is ALSO the canonical home of the portable-protocol north star spec itself (specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md) and the investigation charter (specs/AFI_PROTOCOL_INVESTIGATION_PROMPT.md), but those are documents, not enforced artifacts; the repo as a whole is DOCS. It contains a mix of current direction-setting docs and several stale/contradictory legacy docs flagged by the spec.
primary_purpose: Documentation hub for AFI Protocol: hosts the portable-protocol surface spec and investigation charter, plus developer guides, architecture/status docs, branch/audit reports, droid instructions, and the Signal-Lore narrative series; contains no protocol code.
normative_artifacts: specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md (direction-setting normative-vs-reference charter; the audit north star itself)

reference_only_assumptions.quote:
### 1. afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI
All canonical pipelines, DAGs, and routing logic live here—not in afi-core, not in random helpers.
Repository: afi-reactor  Purpose: Canonical orchestration principles for AFI Protocol  Status: Authoritative

### 3. The DAG is law
Every signal path (ingest → enrich → score → mint/review) must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns.
afi-reactor: TSSD vault persistence via src/services/tssdVaultService.ts; writes canonical USS + pipeline outputs when AFI_MONGO_URI configured
Purpose: Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today.

contradictions.title: Orchestrator Doctrine declares reactor THE protocol orchestrator and is labeled Authoritative, contradicting reference-only framing
contradictions.detail: A full copy of AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md lives in afi-docs root, headed 'Status: Authoritative' and 'This doctrine is authoritative.' It states afi-reactor 'is the orchestrator of AFI' and 'The DAG is law' (every signal path MUST be a Reactor DAG). The portable-protocol spec explicitly lists this doctrine as a tension to reframe: reactor should be the REFERENCE orchestrator, with protocol law being conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions. Having an 'Authoritative' reactor-only doctrine in the docs hub directly works against the portable direction.
contradictions.evidence: AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:5,11,38-40,290 'Status: Authoritative'; '#1 afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'; '#3 The DAG is law ... must be expressible as a Reactor DAG'; 'This doctrine is authoritative. All afi-reactor code must comply.' vs specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:133 'Reframe: reactor is reference orchestrator'

contradictions.title: Pipeline audit presents MongoDB TSSD vault as the canonical/working evidence store
contradictions.detail: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md repeatedly equates the TSSD vault with MongoDB ('MongoDB TSSD vault persistence (Phase 1 complete)', 'afi-infra — TSSD vault (MongoDB)', 'Storage: MongoDB time-series collection', AFI_MONGO_URI). This is Mongo tunnel-vision: the portable spec's vault.schema.json explicitly allows PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB and warns Mongo-only is wrong if it is the only layer. The doc never frames Mongo as a default reference engine.
contradictions.evidence: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:16,41,118 '✅ MongoDB TSSD vault persistence'; 'afi-infra — TSSD vault (MongoDB)'; 'Storage: MongoDB time-series collection' vs specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:52,57 'Mongo, PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, InfluxDB ... Mongo-only and BQ-only are both wrong'

contradictions.title: AFI_Repository_Map.md uses stale/nonexistent repo names (afi-pipeline, afi-agents)
contradictions.detail: The repository map documents 'afi-pipeline' (Signal processing pipeline with DAG execution) and 'afi-agents' (collection of AFI agent implementations) as core repos with interdependency diagrams and a development workflow. The portable spec flags these exact names as stale ('historical afi-pipeline, afi-agents references in older docs'); the current repo census (AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md) lists neither. This misleads new validators on the actual repo topology.
contradictions.evidence: AFI_Repository_Map.md:24-32,54-61,168,170 'afi-pipeline ... DAG execution engine'; 'afi-agents ... agent implementations'; 'afi-gateway → afi-pipeline → afi-reactor' vs specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:181 'historical afi-pipeline, afi-agents references in older docs'

contradictions.title: ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md claims reactor is the single source of truth for orchestration implemented today
contradictions.detail: ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md (dated 2026-06-03, same day as the portable spec) positions afi-reactor as THE deterministic orchestrator and 'single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today,' without the reference-vs-normative caveat. It is more current/accurate than the doctrine (correctly notes custom TS DAG, not LangGraph) but still reinforces reactor-centric framing in a doc-hub status page.
contradictions.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4,10 'Single source of truth for how AFI orchestration is implemented today.'; 'AFI Reactor (afi-reactor) is a custom, deterministic TypeScript DAG orchestrator.'

contradictions.title: Market/analytics plane (BigQuery/Kafka/warehouses/Mage) entirely absent from docs hub
contradictions.detail: Grep for bigquery|kafka|warehouse|data lake|mage|pub/sub across all docs returns zero hits outside the portable spec and investigation prompt. The portable spec names a Market/analytics plane (BigQuery, Kafka/PubSub, data lakes) as one of the five separated planes and Mage as a conforming DAG option. The architecture/status/pipeline docs describe a Mongo+reactor+BASE spine with no analytics plane, reinforcing collapse toward a single store/stack.
contradictions.evidence: grep bigquery|kafka|warehouse|mage across *.md = 0 hits outside specs/; AFI_Full_Architecture.md & AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md describe only Mongo vault + reactor DAG + BASE vs specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:54 'Market / analytics ... BigQuery, Kafka/Pub/Sub, data lakes'

contradictions.title: Internal doc-hierarchy conflict: README/AGENTS/Architecture-Status/Doctrine each claim authority over the others
contradictions.detail: README.md:14 says 'If AGENTS.md conflicts with this README, AGENTS.md wins.' AGENTS.md:25 says 'Codex + AOS are truth sources. Whitepaper is narrative, not canonical.' ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4 calls itself the 'single source of truth.' AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:290 says it is 'authoritative.' No doc points to AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as the top of the hierarchy, so the actual north star is not asserted as canonical inside the hub that hosts it.
contradictions.evidence: README.md:14 'AGENTS.md wins'; AGENTS.md:25 'Codex + AOS are truth sources'; ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:4 'Single source of truth'; AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:290 'This doctrine is authoritative.'

upstream: afi-config (schemas referenced: USS, vault, pipeline, blueprint, plugin-manifest)
afi-reactor (orchestrator doctrine, replay spec, DAG referenced)
afi-core / afi-math / afi-gateway (described as spine components)

recommended_action: tag stale + clarify reference-vs-normative: (1) Add a banner to AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md, ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md, AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md, AFI_System_Atlas.md marking them as REFERENCE-implementation descriptions, not protocol law; reframe 'reactor is THE orchestrator' / 'DAG is law' / 'MongoDB TSSD vault' per the portable spec. (2) Archive or correct AFI_Repository_Map.md (stale afi-pipeline/afi-agents names). (3) Establish explicit doc hierarchy declaring specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md as the top normative reference. (4) Add a Market/analytics plane note. (5) Repair the broken SUMMARY.md sitemap (references many nonexistent files).

notable_findings.title: afi-docs is the canonical home of the portable-protocol north star and the investigation charter
notable_findings.evidence: specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:1-21 'AFI Portable Protocol Surface v0.1 ... distinguish normative rules from reference implementations'; README.md:43 links it as 'Portable protocol direction (normative vs reference)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as the authoritative direction doc; ensure all other architecture docs in this repo defer to it.

notable_findings.title: AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md (reactor-only, 'Authoritative') duplicated into docs hub
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:3-5,11,38-40,290 'Repository: afi-reactor ... Status: Authoritative'; 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'; 'The DAG is law'; 'This doctrine is authoritative. All afi-reactor code must comply.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Reframe as reference orchestration per portable spec; protocol law is conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions, not the reactor codebase.

notable_findings.title: MongoDB equated with TSSD vault across pipeline/atlas docs (Mongo tunnel vision)
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_PIPELINE_AUDIT_REPORT.md:16,41,118,643-666,931 'MongoDB TSSD vault'; 'afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts'; AFI_System_Atlas.md:65 'Multiple Mongo/vault implementations ... needs harmonization'
notable_findings.recommendation: Label Mongo as default reference vault engine; cite vault.schema.json multi-engine support (PG/Timescale/Influx).

notable_findings.title: Stale repo names afi-pipeline and afi-agents documented as core repos
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_Repository_Map.md:24-32 'afi-pipeline ... DAG execution engine'; :54-61 'afi-agents ... agent implementations'; :168 'afi-gateway → afi-pipeline → afi-reactor'
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale; reconcile with AFI_REPO_CENSUS.md (which lists neither) and the portable spec repo inventory.

notable_findings.title: Analytics plane (BigQuery/Kafka/Mage/warehouse) absent from all hub architecture docs
notable_findings.evidence: grep 'bigquery|kafka|warehouse|data lake|mage|pub/sub' over *.md returns 0 hits outside specs/AFI_PROTOCOL_INVESTIGATION_PROMPT.md and AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md
notable_findings.recommendation: Document the optional Market/analytics plane and its integration boundaries (one of the five separated planes).

notable_findings.title: On-chain layer correctly described as lightweight receipt/event (not full ledger) in AFI_Full_Architecture.md
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_Full_Architecture.md:416-425 'AFIMintCoordinator.coordinateMint(beneficiary, amount, signalId, epochId, metadata) ... Emits MintCoordinated(...)'; :429-445 ERC-1155 receipt metadata (signalId/epochId/amount)
notable_findings.recommendation: Consistent with the breadcrumb-not-payload reality; no fix needed, but note no payload/content-hash anchor is documented (aspirational gap).

notable_findings.title: Single-beneficiary mint model documented; no econ splits/gauge present (consistent with current reality)
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_Full_Architecture.md:401,421 'mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount)'; 'coordinateMint(beneficiary, amount, signalId, epochId, metadata)' — single beneficiary; grep for split|gauge|distribut in arch/audit docs = 0 hits
notable_findings.recommendation: None; econ-split tension lives in afi-econ, not documented here. Note absence so cross-repo synthesis flags afi-econ vs single-beneficiary mismatch.

notable_findings.title: LangGraph-era docs are correctly archived and clearly marked superseded
notable_findings.evidence: archive/langgraph-migration-2025/README.md:1-9 'superseded planning ... AFI does not use LangChain, LangGraph...'; ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:46-52 'archived and superseded ... Do not treat archived files as current implementation guidance.'; git log 875fe34 'docs: archive superseded LangGraph architecture notes'
notable_findings.recommendation: Good hygiene model; apply the same explicit-archive banner pattern to the orchestrator doctrine and repository map.

notable_findings.title: Replay/determinism described as reactor-owned and Codex-based; no normative replay rules defined here
notable_findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md:10,14 'custom, deterministic TypeScript DAG orchestrator'; 'Codex configuration and replay support auditability and reproducibility'; AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:80-92 '#6 State & replay belong here ... owned by afi-reactor'
notable_findings.recommendation: Replay invariants are normatively owned by afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md; docs here only narrate them — no replay-critical artifacts stored in this repo.

notable_findings.title: Broken SUMMARY.md sitemap references many nonexistent files
notable_findings.evidence: SUMMARY.md lists faq.md, guides/dev_setup.md, guides/validator.md, guides/factory.md, specs/architecture.md, specs/modules.md, specs/tokenomics.md, specs/governance.md — none exist in the tree (only guides/quickstart.md, specs/{mentor_protocol,modal,AFI_PORTABLE_...,AFI_PROTOCOL_INVESTIGATION...}.md present)
notable_findings.recommendation: Fix or prune SUMMARY.md and README Reference Index links (e.g. README.md:47-48 link to guides/validator.md and guides/factory.md which are missing).

notable_findings.title: AGENTS.md asserts 'Codex + AOS are truth sources; Whitepaper is narrative, not canonical' and overrides README
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:4 'If AGENTS.md conflicts with README or docs, AGENTS.md wins.'; :25 'Codex + AOS are truth sources. Whitepaper is narrative, not canonical.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Reconcile authority hierarchy so the portable-protocol surface spec is acknowledged as the top normative direction doc; AGENTS.md governs editing behavior, not protocol semantics.
